evals.prompt: I have a Next.js app with Playwright tests in e2e/tests/. I want to see what user journeys exist and which ones have test coverage. Can you map the app and show me a coverage diagram?
expected_behavior: Detects Playwright as the UI test framework
Crawls app/ directory for routes and screens
Creates journeys.json with discovered journeys and tested/untested status
Generates Mermaid diagrams with green/amber/red nodes showing coverage

evals.prompt: I have an iOS SwiftUI app and I want to know what's not tested. The project has some XCUITests but I'm not sure how much coverage we have.
expected_behavior: Detects XCUITest as the UI test framework
Finds NavigationStack/TabView patterns to identify screens
Scans existing XCUITest files to determine which screens are covered
Creates journeys.json with tested/untested status per step

evals.prompt: We already have a pathfinder/journeys.json from last sprint. I just want to regenerate the diagrams and see our progress since the baseline.
expected_behavior: Does NOT re-run /map or re-crawl the codebase
Runs generate-diagrams.py with the existing journeys.json
Shows before/after comparison using the saved baseline
Outputs a coverage delta table showing per-journey changes

evals.prompt: I have a monorepo with 3 modules: web (Playwright), ios (XCUITest), and android (Espresso). I want a combined coverage view across all of them.
expected_behavior: Discovers all 3 modules in the monorepo
Runs pathfinder-init.py and /map in each module separately
Runs aggregate.py to merge coverage from all modules into a combined view
Produces a summary showing per-module and overall coverage percentages

evals.prompt: We already mapped our journeys last week. Just write tests for the gaps, don't re-map anything.
expected_behavior: Does NOT run /map or re-crawl the codebase
Reads the existing journeys.json to identify untested steps
Runs /scout to generate UI tests for untested steps only
Uses the correct framework detected from config.json or project files

evals.prompt: I have a Flutter app with zero tests. Can you tell me what the coverage looks like and what framework to use?
expected_behavior: Detects Flutter as the UI framework from pubspec.yaml or project structure
Reports 0% coverage since no test files exist
Creates journeys.json with all steps marked as untested
Recommends flutter_test / integration_test as the testing framework

evals.prompt: My project has both a cypress.config.js and a playwright.config.ts. Map the journeys and show coverage.
expected_behavior: Detects both Cypress and Playwright config files
Selects the primary framework based on which has more existing tests or recent usage
Does not crash or produce duplicate results from conflicting configs
Creates journeys.json and diagrams using the chosen primary framework

evals.prompt: Run /blaze on my project. The pathfinder/journeys.json file exists but it contains invalid JSON.
expected_behavior: Attempts to read and parse journeys.json
Detects the malformed JSON and reports a clear error message
Does NOT crash with an unhandled exception or stack trace
Suggests fixing or regenerating journeys.json by running /map

evals.prompt: Run /map on my project. The pathfinder/journeys.json already exists but contains malformed JSON from a previous interrupted run.
expected_behavior: Exits with a non-zero exit code when parsing fails
Prints an error message containing 'invalid JSON' or 'JSON parse error'
Error message includes the file path of the malformed journeys.json
Does NOT overwrite the malformed file without user confirmation
Does NOT produce a Python traceback visible to the user

evals.prompt: I have a brand new project with just a single empty component and no routes or navigation. Run /map to discover journeys.
expected_behavior: Completes without error (exit code 0)
Reports exactly 0 journeys found in the output
Creates a valid journeys.json with an empty journeys array
Does NOT fabricate or hallucinate journeys that do not exist in the source

evals.prompt: Run /scout on my project to generate tests. There is no pathfinder/journeys.json file yet because I have not run /map.
expected_behavior: Exits with a non-zero exit code
Prints an error message stating that journeys.json is missing or not found
Error message suggests running /map first to generate journeys.json
Does NOT attempt to generate tests without journey data
